q: In Grade 11, the angular displacement, angular velocity and angular acceleration of an object in circular motion have been ___.
In this chapter, the concepts and equations for rotational motion under constant angular acceleration and ___ acceleration will be studied.
In a circular motion, the object just moves in a ___.
Artificial satellites going around the earth at a constant height are an example of ___ motion.
In rotational motion, the object rotates about an ___.
Rotation of earth about its own axis is an example of ___ motion.
Motion of the blades of a fan is an example of ___ motion.
Motion of a Ferris wheel in an amusement park is an example of ___ motion.
A rigid body is a body that does not ___ or change shape.
No matter how a rigid body moves, the distance between any two particles within the body remains ___.
If a rigid body is in rotational motion, all the particles constituting it undergo circular motion about a common ___.
When an object (rigid body) rotates, it can speed up (or) slow ___.
During time intervals when an object rotates, its angular velocity is changing therefore it has an angular ___.
If its angular velocity changes at a constant rate, then the angular acceleration is ___.
The motion where angular acceleration is constant is called the rotational motion under constant angular ___.
Rotational variables such as angular displacement, angular velocity, and angular acceleration have been introduced ___.
In this section, rotational variables are applied to analyze the rotational motion for a rigid body about a fixed axis under a ___ angular acceleration.

For an object that rotates with a constant angular acceleration, the kinematic equations for rotational motion can be derived in terms of the angular displacement, angular velocity and angular acceleration of the object at any ___ of time.

If the initial angular speed (magnitude of angular velocity of the object at $t = 0$) is $\omega_0$, its angular speed $\omega$ at time $t$ can be derived from the constant angular acceleration $\alpha = \frac{\omega - \omega_0}{___}$.
The equation $\omega = \omega_0 + \alpha t$ relates final angular velocity, initial angular velocity, angular acceleration, and ___.
Average angular speed is calculated as $\bar{\omega} = \frac{\omega + ___}{2}$.
Angular displacement for time interval $t$ is $\theta = \bar{\omega}___$.
Angular displacement $\theta$ and angular speed $\omega$ can be expressed by equations using Eq. (1.1) and Eq. ___.
One equation for angular displacement is $\theta = \omega_0t + \frac{1}{2}\alpha ___^2$.
Another equation relating angular speed and displacement is $\omega^2 = \omega_0^2 + 2\alpha ___$.
The equations of motion for constant angular acceleration are the same as those for ___ motion.
In rotational motion, angular quantities are replaced with corresponding ___ ones in the equations for constant angular acceleration.
The equations for constant angular acceleration in rotational motion are analogous to equations for constant acceleration in ___ motion.
